mechanism_narrative: Sustained low unemployment indicates a strong labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of periods where UNRATE is below its 10-year average.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above a certain threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation, which typically dampens economic activity and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales and increasing housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and investment, which are key drivers for corporate revenue growth and positive equity market performance over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 5%
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict positive 20d returns with a precision of at least 0.6 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low industrial production growth, a further deceleration signals weakening manufacturing and overall economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances during identified slowdown regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in GDP reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which generally translates into higher corporate earnings and positive sentiment for equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of periods where GDPC1 growth is above its 5-year average.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPI, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, prompting central bank tightening and leading to negative equity market performance over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances when inflation exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong employment growth (PAYEMS) and low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer confidence and spending, which drives positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict positive 60d returns with a precision of at least 0.6 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong consumer spending, as indicated by retail sales, a significant deceleration signals a loss of consumer confidence and potential economic weakness, leading to negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances during identified consumer spending contraction regimes.
